response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine high-scale distributed systems exposure and a strong testing culture, but the resume shows no experimentation, A/B testing, telemetry, or growth-measurement platform work — the core domain of this role — and no evidence of principal-level, multi-team technical leadership. His trajectory is consistently senior IC on product and full-stack work, which falls short of the IC6 scope of architecting experimentation frameworks used across teams.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second)
Broad polyglot engineering background (TypeScript/Node, DLang, C/C++, Java) with strong craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source test tooling
BS in Computer Science from MIT plus roughly 15 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure
concerns: No experimentation, feature-flagging, metrics/telemetry, or statistical analysis experience anywhere in the resume
No demonstrated principal-level scope: no multi-team initiatives, platform ownership, or cross-org technical leadership
Career has remained at the senior IC level with frequent product-focused roles, suggesting a gap to IC6 expectations in growth/measurement systems

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and scale experience
